action: Click on the Start menu, search for 'Notepad', and open the Notepad application.
fallback_action: Perform a Google search using Internet Explorer

recipe.steps: is the first action. The task is to open Notepad by clicking the Start menu, searching for 'Notepad', and opening it. The Start menu is typically accessed via the Windows icon, which is represented by Box ID 173 in the bottom left corner of the screen. I will click this to begin the process.

previous action: I clicked on the Start menu (Box ID 173). The expected outcome was that the Start menu would open. On the current screen, I can see the Start menu is now open, as evidenced by the presence of app icons and a search bar at the bottom left. The next step is to search for 'Notepad' by clicking into the search bar and typing 'Notepad'. The search bar is visually located at the bottom of the Start menu, but no bounding box is detected for it. I will click in the area where the search bar is located to focus it.

previous action: I clicked in the area where the Start menu search bar is located. The expected outcome was that the search bar would be focused and ready for typing. On the current screen, the Start menu is still open and the search bar area appears visually ready for input. There are no error messages or unexpected changes. The next step is to type 'Notepad' to search for the Notepad application.

previous action: I typed 'Notepad' in the Start menu search bar. The expected outcome was that search results for Notepad would appear. On the current screen, I can see the Notepad application icon and name in the search results area. This confirms the previous action succeeded. The next step is to open Notepad by clicking on its icon in the search results.

previous action: I clicked on the Notepad icon in the Start menu search results. The expected outcome was that the Notepad application would open. On the current screen, however, I do not see the Notepad window open. The screen still shows a browser window and other unrelated content, with no visible Notepad window or text area. This suggests that the previous action did not succeed in opening Notepad, possibly due to a misclick or a delay in launching the application. As a corrective action, I will wait for a moment to see if Notepad opens, in case there is a delay.

previous action: I waited to see if Notepad would open after clicking its icon in the Start menu. The expected outcome was that the Notepad window would appear. However, after waiting, the current screen still does not show the Notepad application. The visible windows are a file explorer dialog and a browser, with no sign of Notepad. This suggests that the previous attempt to open Notepad failed. As a corrective action, I will repeat the process by searching for 'Notepad' again in the Start menu search bar and attempting to open it once more. Since the Start menu is not currently visible, I will click the Windows Start button to reopen it.

previous action: I clicked the Windows Start button to reopen the Start menu. The expected outcome was that the Start menu would appear, allowing me to search for Notepad again. On the current screen, I can see the Start menu is now open, with the search bar visible at the bottom. The next step is to click into the search bar area to focus it so I can type 'Notepad' again.

previous action: I clicked in the area where the Start menu search bar is located. The expected outcome was that the search bar would be focused and ready for typing. The Start menu is open and the search bar area appears visually ready for input. There are no error messages or unexpected changes. The next step is to type 'Notepad' to search for the Notepad application again.

previous action: I typed 'Notepad' in the Start menu search bar. The expected outcome was that search results for Notepad would appear. On the current screen, I can see the Start menu is still open, and the search results area is visible. The Notepad application should be listed in the results, but the bounding boxes do not explicitly label Notepad. However, based on the previous successful attempt, the Notepad icon should be in the search results area. I will look for a likely candidate in the search results area and click it to open Notepad. Since the previous attempt used a box in the search results area, and Box ID 4 is currently highlighted (likely the search bar), I will look for a box in the area just above or near the search bar. Box ID 0 is labeled 'Downloads', which is not Notepad. There is no explicit Notepad label, so I will try clicking in the area above the search bar, which is a common location for the first search result.

previous action: I clicked on Box ID 5, which was in the area above the search bar, hoping it would open Notepad. The expected outcome was that the Notepad application window would appear. However, the current screen still does not show the Notepad window. Instead, the file explorer window and other unrelated content are still visible, and there is no sign of Notepad being launched. This suggests that the previous click did not successfully open Notepad. As a corrective action, I will try clicking on another likely candidate in the search results area. Box ID 4 is labeled as 'WPS PDF Document', which is not Notepad, but Box ID 6 is labeled 'Hot days ahead 38"C', which is also not Notepad. Since the search results may not be showing Notepad, I will try scrolling up in case the result is out of view.

previous action: I attempted to scroll up in the Start menu search results area to see if the Notepad application would appear. The expected outcome was that new search results, possibly including Notepad, would become visible. However, after scrolling up, the visible content on the screen does not show any new search results or the Notepad application. The same set of unrelated content is still visible, and there is no indication that Notepad is present in the search results. As a corrective action, I will try typing 'Notepad' again in the search bar to refresh the search results, as the current results do not seem to include Notepad.

previous action: I typed 'Notepad' again in the search bar to refresh the search results. The expected outcome was that the search results would update to show the Notepad application. However, the current screen still does not display Notepad in the search results area. The visible content remains unrelated, and there is no indication that Notepad is present. Since repeated attempts to search for Notepad from the Start menu have failed, I will try an alternative approach: opening Notepad using the Run dialog. I will press the Windows + R keys to open the Run dialog, but since keyboard shortcuts are not allowed, I will instead right-click the Start menu to see if the 'Run' option is available.

previous action: I right-clicked the Start menu to open the context menu, expecting to see options such as 'Run'. The current screen shows the right-click context menu with several options, including 'Run' (Box ID 47). This confirms the previous action succeeded. The next step is to left-click on the 'Run' option to open the Run dialog.

previous action: I left-clicked on the 'Run' option in the Start menu context menu (Box ID 47). The expected outcome was that the Run dialog would appear. On the current screen, I can see the Run dialog is now open, as indicated by the small window with a text input field and 'Open' and 'Cancel' buttons. The next step is to type 'notepad' into the text input field of the Run dialog to launch Notepad.

previous action: I typed 'notepad' into the text input field of the Run dialog. The expected outcome was that the text 'notepad' would appear in the input field. On the current screen, I can see the Run dialog with 'notepad' correctly entered in the input field. The next step is to launch Notepad by clicking the 'OK' button in the Run dialog.

previous action: I clicked the 'OK' button in the Run dialog after entering 'notepad'. The expected outcome was that the Notepad application would launch. On the current screen, I can see the Notepad window is now open, with its familiar blank text area and title bar. This confirms the previous action succeeded. The next step is to click in the main text area of Notepad to ensure it is focused and ready for typing.
